messages.content:
Question: What is the title of the sports article whose description has the greatest number of characters?

Available databases: articles_db, metadata_database

Available schema:
Database: articles_db (mongodb)
Database: metadata_database (sqlite)
  Table: authors — author_id, name
  Table: article_metadata — article_id, author_id, region, publication_date

function.name: execute_python
function.arguments:
{"code": "import json

data = env['data_2']
for i, row in enumerate(data[:20]):
    title = row.get('title', '')
    desc = row.get('description', '')
    print(f"{i}: {title}")
    if 'sport' in desc.lower() or 'game' in desc.lower() or 'team' in desc.lower() or 'coach' in desc.lower() or 'player' in desc.lower() or 'season' in desc.lower() or 'league' in desc.lower() or 'champion' in desc.lower() or 'olympic' in desc.lower() or 'tournament' in desc.lower() or 'match' in desc.lower() or 'cup' in desc.lower():
        print(f"  -> Potential Sports: {desc[:100]}")
"}

function.name: execute_python
function.arguments:
{"code": "import json

data = env['data_2']

sports_keywords = [
    'sport', 'game', 'team', 'coach', 'player', 'season', 'league', 'champion', 
    'olympic', 'tournament', 'match', 'cup', 'baseball', 'basketball', 'football', 
    'soccer', 'tennis', 'golf', 'hockey', 'nfl', 'nba', 'mlb', 'nhl', 'fifa', 'uefa',
    'stadium', 'athlete', 'medal', 'pitcher', 'quarterback', 'striker', 'wimbledon',
    'nascar', 'racing', 'wrestling', 'boxing', 'marathon', 'rugby', 'cricket'
]

business_keywords = ['ibm', 'sybase', 'linux', 'database', 'software', 'server', 'market', 'profit', 'company', 'business', 'stock', 'shares', 'investor', 'revenue', 'ceo', 'executive', 'enterprise', 'telecom', 'broadband', 'partnership']
tech_keywords = ['rss', 'it', 'network', 'protocol', 'bandwidth', 'slashdot', 'blogger', 'ip telephony', 'vpn', 'voip', 'pbx', 'desktop', 'backup', 'internet', 'software', 'microsoft', 'intel', 'nvidia', 'chipset']
world_keywords = ['senate', 'bush', 'kerry', 'court', 'supreme court', 'kyoto', 'india', 'delaware', 'government', 'election', 'president', 'minister', 'war', 'military', 'treaty']

for row in data:
    title = row.get('title', '')
    desc = row.get('description', '')
    text = (title + " " + desc).lower()
    
    sports_score = sum(1 for k in sports_keywords if k in text)
    biz_score = sum(1 for k in business_keywords if k in text)
    tech_score = sum(1 for k in tech_keywords if k in text)
    world_score = sum(1 for k in world_keywords if k in text)
    
    if sports_score > biz_score and sports_score > tech_score and sports_score > world_score and sports_score > 0:
        print(f"Title: {title}")
        print(f"Desc: {desc}")
        print(f"Length: {row.get('desc_len')}")
        print(f"Scores - Sports: {sports_score}, Biz: {biz_score}, Tech: {tech_score}, World: {world_score}")
        print("-" * 50)
        break
"}

messages.name: execute_python
messages.content:
Execution successful. Output:
Title: The Rundown
Desc: 4 Miami at N.C. State <em>7:45 p.m., ESPN </em><br>Think the Wolfpack is kicking itself for that loss two weeks ago at North Carolina? You bet. Had N.C. State (4-2, 3-1 ACC) won that one, this would be for sole possession of first place in the ACC. As it is, this is a chance for the Wolfpack to show it belongs in the upper echelon of the restructured league -- which, for now, is Miami, Florida State, and a cesspool of also-rans. The Wolfpack's defense is the best in the nation against the pass (97.5 yards per game) and overall (203.7). It will have to shut down a rejuvenated Brock Berlin, who threw for 308 yards last week against Louisville, his most in 13 games. Key for N.C. State: Will perpetually banged-up tailback T.A. McLendon -- a game-time decision because of a bad hamstring -- be able to run effectively?
Length: 841
Scores - Sports: 2, Biz: 0, Tech: 1, World: 0
--------------------------------------------------
